faq.a: Use the form above with the subject "Submit a mix". Include a SoundCloud, Mixcloud or WeTransfer link along with your tracklist.

faq.q: How to become an artist on DarkVolt?
faq.a: Send us your demo and a link to your work. Our programming team will respond within 5 business days.

faq.q: How to exercise my GDPR rights?
faq.a: Send an email to rgpd@darkvolt.fr stating your identity and the nature of your request. Response time: 30 days.
Try refreshing the page or clearing the cache. If the problem persists, contact us via the form by selecting "Technical support".

submit.title: Submit a Mix
submit.subtitle: You produce underground electronic music? Join the DarkVolt roster. Our team listens to every submission carefully.

steps.title: Submit your mix
steps.desc: Fill in the form below with the link to your mix (SoundCloud, Mixcloud, WeTransfer) and your full tracklist.

steps.title: Listen & selection
steps.desc: Our team listens to every submission. Criteria: technical quality, originality, consistency with DarkVolt programming.

steps.title: Response within 5 days
steps.desc: You'll receive a reply by email within 5 business days — accepted with broadcast date, or a motivated rejection.

steps.title: Live broadcast
steps.desc: If selected, your mix is scheduled on the web radio with announcement on our networks. You officially join the roster.

submit.mixUrlHint: SoundCloud, Mixcloud, WeTransfer, Dropbox… any publicly accessible link.
submit.bioPh: A few lines about your project, history, style...
submit.agreement: I agree that DarkVolt may broadcast my mix on its web radio and social networks for non-commercial purposes. I certify I hold the necessary rights to the works contained in this mix. I have read the

faq.q: Does my mix need to be unreleased?
faq.a: No, it can have been released on other platforms. What matters is quality and consistency with our editorial line.

faq.q: Am I paid for the broadcast?
faq.a: DarkVolt is an underground web radio with no commercial model at this stage. Broadcasting is unpaid but guaranteed maximum coverage.
Yes, but one submission at a time. Wait for a response on your first submission before sending another.

faq.q: What link formats do you accept?
faq.a: SoundCloud, Mixcloud, WeTransfer, Dropbox, Google Drive… any link allowing direct listening or download.

faq.q: My mix was rejected, what now?
faq.a: A rejection is always motivated. You can resubmit a new mix after 30 days. Feedback is constructive, not final.

faq.q: Can I join the roster without a mix?
faq.a: For now, the main route is mix submission. Occasional partnerships are possible — contact us via Discord.

submit.errorMissingDesc: Please fill in all required fields.
submit.successDesc: Our team will contact you within 5 business days.

cgu.title: Terms of Service
cgu.subtitle: By accessing the DarkVolt platform, you accept these terms in their entirety. Please read them carefully before any use.

preamble.title: Preamble
preamble.p1a: These Terms of Service govern access to and use of the
preamble.p1b: platform, accessible at darkvolt.fr and operated by [TO BE COMPLETED].
preamble.p2: Any use of the Platform implies full acceptance of these ToS. The Publisher reserves the right to modify the ToS at any time.

art1.title: Article 1 — Service description
art1.intro: DarkVolt is a webradio specializing in underground electronic music (Techno, Industrial, Dark Electro, Hardtek, etc.). The Platform offers:
items: A real-time audio broadcast stream (live webradio)
Recorded music programs and shows
Podcasts and archives for on-demand listening
A community space (chat, forum, artist profiles)
Information on events and concerts
art1.note: Access to certain features may require the creation of a user account.

art2.title: Article 2 — Platform access
art2.p21: The Platform is freely accessible to any user with internet access. All access costs are the exclusive responsibility of the user.
art2.p22: The Publisher strives to ensure Platform availability 24/7. Interruptions may occur for maintenance or force majeure.
art2.p23: Certain features require account creation. The user agrees to provide accurate information and keep it up to date. Each user is responsible for the confidentiality of their credentials.
art2.p24a: The minimum age required to register is
art2.p24b: years. Minors under 15 must obtain parental consent per GDPR Article 8.

art3.title: Article 3 — User obligations
art3.intro: By using the Platform, the user agrees to:
items: Comply with applicable laws and regulations
Not publish unlawful, defamatory, racist or hateful content
Not impersonate another person or represent DarkVolt
Not attempt unauthorized access to the Platform's systems
Not distribute viruses, malware or harmful code
Not use bots or scrapers to collect data
Not harass, threaten or intimidate other users
Respect third-party intellectual property rights

art4.title: Article 4 — User content
art4.p41: The user is solely responsible for the content they publish. By publishing, the user guarantees they hold all necessary rights.
art4.p42: By submitting content, the user grants the Publisher a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license for the duration of copyright protection.
art4.p43: The Publisher reserves the right to remove, without notice, any unlawful content or content contrary to these ToS.

art5.title: Article 5 — Intellectual property
art5.p1: All Platform elements (design, code, logo, texts, sounds, images) are protected by the Intellectual Property Code.
art5.p2: The Publisher pays fees to collective management organizations (SACEM, SCPP, SPPF). Downloading or redistributing broadcast content without authorization is strictly prohibited.

art6.title: Article 6 — Suspension and termination
art6.intro: The Publisher may suspend or terminate a user's access in case of:
items: Violation of these ToS
Fraudulent or abusive behavior
Provision of inaccurate registration information
Non-compliance with applicable legislation
Substantiated report from another user
art6.notea: The user may delete their account at any time from their profile settings or by contacting:

art7.title: Article 7 — Publisher liability
art7.intro: The Platform is provided "as is". The Publisher cannot be held liable for:
items: Direct or indirect damages from use or inability to use the Platform
Content published by third-party users
Service interruptions related to force majeure
Data loss due to technical failures
Actions of third parties on the Platform

art8.title: Article 8 — Personal data
art8.p1a: Personal data processing is governed by DarkVolt's

art9.title: Article 9 — Applicable law and jurisdiction
art9.p1: These ToS are governed by French law. Any dispute will be submitted to the competent courts of France.
art9.p2a: Any consumer may resort free of charge to a consumer mediator. You can also access the EU online dispute resolution platform:

confidentialite.title: Privacy Policy
confidentialite.subtitle: DarkVolt is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy describes how we collect, use and protect your personal data in accordance with the GDPR.

s1.title: 1. Data Controller
s1.intro: The controller of personal data collected via darkvolt.fr is:
items: Identity: [TO BE COMPLETED]
Address: [TO BE COMPLETED]
Email: contact@darkvolt.fr
DPO: [TO BE COMPLETED or not designated]
s1.p1: For any questions regarding your personal data, contact us at the above email address.

s2.title: 2. Data Collected
items21: Selected role: listener, guest artist or streamer
items22: Browsing data and pages visited (anonymized analytics)
Messages sent in the community chat
Mixes and content submitted to the platform
s2.h23: Data collected via the contact form
s2.p24: During browsing, technical data is automatically collected: IP address, browser, OS, pages visited, visit durations. Used for statistical and security purposes.

s3.title: 3. Purposes and Legal Bases of Processing
s3.intro: In accordance with Article 6 of the GDPR, each processing has an identified legal basis:

s4.title: 4. Data Retention
items: Active account data: duration of registration, then 3 years after last activity
Chat messages: 1 rolling year, then automatic deletion
Connection and security logs: 12 months
Contact form data: 3 years from last interaction
Analytics cookies: max 13 months (CNIL recommendation)
Billing data (if applicable): 10 years (accounting obligation)
s4.p1: Upon expiry of these periods, your data is deleted or irreversibly anonymized.

s5.title: 5. Data Recipients
s5.p51: Your data may be transmitted to sub-processors strictly necessary for the service, bound by GDPR Article 28 compliant contracts:
items51: Transactional email service: [TO BE COMPLETED if applicable]
Audience analytics: [TO BE COMPLETED — e.g. self-hosted Matomo, Plausible…]
s5.p52: DarkVolt strives to use EU-based providers. Any transfer outside the EU is governed by appropriate safeguards per GDPR Chapter V.
s5.p53: Your data may be communicated to French judicial or administrative authorities when required by law.

s6.title: 6. Cookies and Trackers
s6.p1: The Platform uses cookies. Non-essential cookies are only placed with your prior consent.
itemsNecessary: Session cookie: maintaining your connection (duration: session)
GDPR preference cookie: storing consent choices (duration: 6 months)
Authentication token (duration: 30 days if stay logged in)
itemsConsent: Audience analytics cookies: anonymized traffic measurement
Personalization cookies: adapting content to listening preferences
s6.p2: You can manage cookie preferences at any time via your browser settings or our consent manager.

s7.title: 7. Your Rights
s7.p1: Under GDPR Articles 15–22, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
items: Right of access (Art. 15): obtain a copy of your data
Right of rectification (Art. 16): correct inaccurate or incomplete data
Right to erasure (Art. 17): request deletion of your data
Right to restriction (Art. 18): request suspension of processing
Right to data portability (Art. 20): receive your data in a structured format
Right to object (Art. 21): object to processing based on legitimate interest
Right to withdraw consent at any time (Art. 7)
Right not to be subject to automated decision-making (Art. 22)
s7.pExercicea: Send your request by email to
s7.pExerciseb: specifying your identity and request. We will respond within
s7.pExercisec: (extendable by two months for complex requests).

s8.title: 8. Complaint to the CNIL
s8.p1: If you believe your data processing does not comply with regulations, you may lodge a complaint with:
items: Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés (CNIL)
3 Place de Fontenoy — TSA 80715 — 75334 PARIS CEDEX 07
Website: https://www.cnil.fr
s8.p2: We encourage you to contact us first so we can address your concern directly.

s9.title: 9. Data Security
s9.p1: The Publisher implements appropriate technical and organizational measures per GDPR Article 32. These include:
items: Password encryption using bcrypt
Data transmission via HTTPS (TLS)
Data access restricted to authorized personnel
Access logging and anomaly monitoring
Regular encrypted backups

s4.p2: The publisher declines liability for any inaccuracy or damages resulting from fraudulent third-party intrusion.
s4.pLinks: The site may contain links to other websites. The publisher has no control over them and is not responsible for their content.

s7.title: 7. Audiovisual Broadcasting
s7.p1: DarkVolt is an online music broadcasting platform complying with applicable audiovisual communication regulations, in particular:
items: Law No. 86-1067 of September 30, 1986 on freedom of communication
ARCOM decrees and recommendations
Prior declaration to ARCOM for online radio services if applicable
Compliance with French-language music broadcast quotas
